summary: On July 17th at approximately 1845 hours, Alaska State Troopers responded to a suspicious circumstances call on Nordale Road. sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Slow growth seen in the Goldrun Complex
summary: Update acreage for the nine fires in the Goldrun Complex has been hard to obtain due to persistent cloud cover; however, members of the Wyoming Type 3 IMT were able to take flight Thursday to map the fires from the air. At a combined acreage of 33,703 acres, the fires have seen minimal growth over t...

title: AST Pursuit: Palmer [AK25070820]
summary: On 7/18/2025 AST located a vehicle driving recklessly on the Palmer-Wasilla Hwy heading towards Palmer. The vehicle was swerving in between lanes and showed signs of an impaired driver. Before any Law Enforcement contact by AST the vehicle was deemed a risk to public safety. AST and Palmer Police at...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Tlozhavun Fire remains 50% contained as crews advance mop up
summary: The Tlozhavun Fire (#448) remains at 21 acres and 50% contained, with crews working towards 50-foot mop up operations, targeting residual heat, and preparing for warmer, drier conditions while continuing full suppression efforts. Tlozhavun Fire (#448) – The Tlozhavun Fire (#448), located approximate...

summary: Area map of the of the 7 Mile Lookout Fire and the Tok River Fire for July 18, 2025. Investigation revealed Fanene, failed to record her catch on her Upper Cook Inlet Personal User Permit. Fanene was issued a citation with a fine of $120...

title: AST Personal Use Fishery Violation: Kenai North Beach [AK25070622]
summary: On 7/18/2025 Alaska Wildlife Troopers contacted Matthew Kam-Magruder, age 50, of Anchorage, while dipnet fishing in the Kenai River Dipnet Fishery on Kenai North Beach. Investigation revealed Kam-Magruder had taken a King Salmon while dipnet fishing. Kam-Magruder was issued a citation with a $270 fi...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Senate AFN is Happy to Share Exciting News from the Denali Commission
summary: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE July 17, 2025 ANCHORAGE, AK – In response to critical and urgent infrastructure needs in rural Alaska, the Denali Commission has awarded a landmark $100 million federal financial assistance grant to the Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium (ANTHC). The funding, transferred from the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and authorized under the Clean Air Act, will support major upgrades to bulk fuel tank farms in ten rural communities facing significant energy and environmental risks. This historic investment represents the largest single-year allocation for bulk fuel facility improvements in Alaska in more...

title: NEWS: Myalgic Encephalomyelitis Awareness Day To Be Recognized in Alaska
summary: ANCHORAGE, AK — Earlier this week, Senate Bill 60 , sponsored by Senator Löki Tobin (D-Anchorage), officially became law without the Governor’s signature, which establishes May 12 as Myalgic Encephalomyelitis (ME) Awareness Day in Alaska. The post NEWS: Myalgic Encephalomyelitis Awareness Day To Be...

title: Minimal fire activity observed across the Ptarmigan Complex, incident management team prepares for transition
summary: Update 4 p.m., July 18, 2025. Thanks to moderated fire activity and successful suppression efforts on the Ptarmigan Complex, the Level 1 “Ready” evacuation notice was lifted for the Central Area. While this is encouraging news, residents are urged to stay alert – fires are still active nearby, and Alaska remains in fire season. Residents can take simple steps to reduce wildfire risk: move flammable items like firewood, ATVs, and gas cans at least 30 feet from homes; clear needles, leaves, and brush from roofs, decks, and yards; trim grass and low branches; and keep the first 5 feet around y...

title: US House votes to yank funding for NPR, PBS, foreign aid, sending bill to Trump’s desk
summary: The U.S. Capitol on July 2, 2025. (Photo by Ashley Murray/States Newsroom) WASHINGTON — The U.S. House cleared legislation just after midnight Friday that will cancel $9 billion in previously approved spending for public broadcasting and foreign aid, marking only the second time in more than three decades Congress has approved a presidential rescissions request. The 216-213 mostly party-line vote sends the bill to President Donald Trump for his signature and notches another legislative victory for the White House, following passage earlier in July of a giant ta...

title: Firefighters continue to prepare and protect allotments on the north and south fronts of the Roundabout Complex’s Moldy Fire
summary: The Moldy Fire (#279) saw a third day of minimal fire behavior with no growth or movement to report. The Midnight Sun hotshot crew arrived yesterday to prepare and protect allotments in the southern division. The northern division is repositioning to protect allotments and other values to the north...

title: Tracking Medicaid patients’ work status may prove difficult for states
summary: A construction worker sprays water on his face during a break in maintenance work along Pennsylvania Avenue in Washington, D.C. States and the District of Columbia that expanded Medicaid must begin verifying millions of enrollees’ monthly work status by the end of next year — a task some critics say states will have a hard time carrying out. (Photo by Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images) States must begin verifying millions of Medicaid enrollees’ monthly work status by the end of next year — a task some critics say states will have a hard time carrying out. A provision in the tax and spending bill President Donald Trump signed into law July 4 will require the 40 states plus Washington, D.C., that have expanded Medicaid to check paperwork at least twice a year to ensure those enrollees are volunteering or working at least 80 hours a month or attending school at least half time. The new law provides states $200 million for fiscal year 2026 to get their systems up...
